cue: Flipping the subtraction reverses the order the comparator imposes.

prompt: Write `bubbleSort(a)` that sorts `a` ascending in place and returns it by repeatedly swapping adjacent out-of-order pairs. Do not use the built-in sort.
cue: Each pass walks adjacent pairs, swapping to float the largest to the end.

prompt: Write `byLastChar(words)` returning a new array of `words` sorted by their last character in alphabetical order. Equal last characters keep their original order. Do not mutate the input.
cue: Derive each element's last character as the sort key on a copy.

prompt: Write `topK(nums, k)` returning the `k` largest values of `nums` in descending order. Do not mutate the input. If `k` exceeds the length, return all values sorted descending.
cue: Sort a copy descending, then take the first k.
note: Sorting is O(n log n); a heap or quickselect finds the k largest in O(n log k) or O(n) average when n is huge and k is small.
mapsTo: LC 215 · Kth Largest Element in an Array

prompt: Write `minGap(nums)` returning the smallest difference between any two values in `nums` (length at least 2). Sort a copy so the closest pair is adjacent; do not mutate the input.
cue: After sorting, the minimum gap is between neighbors, so sweep adjacent pairs.
note: The closest pair can only be adjacent once sorted, so you check n-1 neighbor gaps instead of all n^2 pairs.
